result.markdown:
---
title: "Install"
---







## Python

The Python package is the source of truth — install it first whether you plan to use Python or R.

```sh
git clone https://github.com/alejandroschuler/rieszboost.git
cd rieszboost
python3 -m venv .venv
.venv/bin/pip install -e python/
```

`xgboost` requires OpenMP. On macOS:

```sh
brew install libomp
```

Linux runners (including GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest) ship `libgomp1` preinstalled, so no extra step is needed there.

### Verify

::: {.panel-tabset group="lang"}

## Python




::: {.cell}

```{.python .cell-code}
import rieszboost
from rieszboost import RieszBooster, ATE
print("rieszboost", "OK")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
rieszboost OK
```

:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}

```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "rieszboost (R wrapper) OK"
```


:::
:::




:::

## R wrapper (optional)

The R package is a thin R6 mirror around the Python implementation via [reticulate](https://rstudio.github.io/reticulate/). It does not require a separate install of any algorithmic dependency — everything runs in the Python venv.

Three steps the first time you use it:

1. Install the R package dependencies (`reticulate`, `R6`):
   ```r
   install.packages(c("reticulate", "R6"))
   ```
2. Either install the R wrapper as a package or dev-load it from the source tree. For this guide we dev-load:
   ```r
   pkgload::load_all("r/rieszboost")
   ```
3. Point reticulate at the Python venv where you installed `rieszboost`:
   ```r
   use_python_rieszboost(".venv/bin/python")
   ```
   You can also set the env var `RETICULATE_PYTHON` before R starts:
   ```sh
   export RETICULATE_PYTHON=/path/to/rieszboost/.venv/bin/python
   ```

After that, every R example in this guide runs verbatim.

::: {.callout-note title="What R covers"}
The R wrapper exports every public Python class except the symbolic tracer for **custom** `m()` (see [estimands](estimands.qmd) and [r-interface](r-interface.qmd)). All built-in estimands, both losses, both backends, and the entire `RieszBooster` API are first-class in R.
:::

## Tests

```{.bash}
.venv/bin/python -m pytest python/tests -v

Rscript -e '
  Sys.setenv(RETICULATE_PYTHON = file.path(getwd(), ".venv/bin/python"))
  pkgload::load_all("r/rieszboost")
  testthat::test_dir("r/rieszboost/tests/testthat")
'
```
